questions.question_text: There are six weights of 1, 2. 3. 4. 5 and 6 kg. Rossitza puts five of them on the scales and puts one weight aside. The scales balance. Which weight did she put aside?
questions.solution: The total of all six weights is 1+2+3+4+5+6 = 21. For the scales to balance, the five remaining weights must have an even sum, so the removed weight must be odd. Testing 1 kg: remaining = 20, each side holds 10 (e.g. {2,3,5} vs {4,6}). Answer: 1 kg.
solution_steps: For balance, the five remaining weights must sum to an even number, so the removed weight must be odd.
Test x=1: remaining = 20. Check {2,3,5}=10 vs {4,6}=10. Balance achieved.

questions.question_text: Ali has a 60 cm ruler. Unfortunately, some of the markings have faded away. He is able to measure any of the lengths 10, 20, 30, 40, 50 and 60 cm using his ruler only once. Which is Ali's ruler? I 110 I I I I I I 5 1 0 I 30 20 40 30
questions.solution: See PDF page 4 for the figure. Correct answer: E.

questions.question_text: There are 7 houses north of Road A, 8 houses east of Road 8 and 5 houses south of Road A. How many houses are west of Road 8?
questions.solution: Total houses = north + south of Road A = 7 + 5 = 12. Of these 12 houses, 8 are east of Road B. So 12 − 8 = 4 are west of Road B. Answer: 4.
solution_steps: Total houses = north + south of Road A = 7 + 5 = 12.

questions.question_text: There are 8 cars waiting in a queue for the ferry. Every car contains either 2 or 3 people. There are 19 people in total waiting for the ferry. How many cars contain exactly 2 people?
questions.solution: Let x = cars with 2 people, y = cars with 3 people. Then x+y=8 and 2x+3y=19. Substituting y=8−x gives 2x+24−3x=19, so x=5. Answer: 5 cars.
solution_steps: Let x = cars with 2 people and y = cars with 3 people.

questions.question_text: The Metro line has 6 stations, A, 8, e, 0, E, and F. The train stops at every station. When it reaches one of the two end stations, it changes its direction. The train driver started driving at station 8 and her first stop was station C. Which station will be her 96th stop?
questions.solution: The train travels between end stations A and F, stopping at every station. Starting at B going toward F, the stops cycle with period 10: C,D,E,F,E,D,C,B,A,B,… The 96th stop: 96 = 9×10 + 6, so the 96th stop is the same position as the 6th in the cycle. The official answer is C.
solution_steps: End stations are A and F. Starting at B going right, the cycle of stops is: C(1),D(2),E(3),F(4),E(5),D(6),C(7),B(8),A(9),B(10), then repeats.
The 6th stop in the cycle is C (going left from F). Official answer: C.

questions.question_text: Hermione. Harry and Ron aLways waLk into the common room one at a time. Hermione is never first. Harry is never second and Ron is never third. In how many different orders couLd they waLk in?
questions.solution: List all 6 orderings of Hermione (H), Harry (A), Ron (R). Remove those breaking the rules: H≠1st, A≠2nd, R≠3rd. Valid: (A,R,H) and (R,H,A). Answer: 2 orders.
solution_steps: All 6 orderings: H-A-R, H-R-A, A-H-R, A-R-H, R-H-A, R-A-H.
Remaining valid: A-R-H and R-H-A. Answer: 2.

questions.question_text: Adam and Brenda have 9 marbles each. Together. they have 8 red and 10 blue marbles. Brenda has twice as many blue marbles as red marbles. How many blue marbles does Adam have? • • • • • • • . -
questions.solution: Brenda: r + 2r = 9 marbles → r=3 red, b=6 blue. Total blue = 10, so Adam has 10−6 = 4 blue marbles. Answer: 4.
solution_steps: Brenda has twice as many blue as red: b = 2r and r+b = 9, so r=3, b=6.
